verses.text: The word of the LORD was rare in those days — a spiritual famine — and visions seldom came. Yet 'the lamp of God' had not gone out, and the boy Samuel slept near the ark. In the darkest seasons God keeps a flame alive and prepares to speak again.

The word of the LORD was rare in those days — a spiritual famine — and visions seldom came. Yet 'the lamp of God' had not gone out, and the boy Samuel slept near the ark. In the darkest seasons God keeps a flame alive and prepares to speak again.

The word of the LORD was rare in those days — a spiritual famine — and visions seldom came. Yet 'the lamp of God' had not gone out, and the boy Samuel slept near the ark. In the darkest seasons God keeps a flame alive and prepares to speak again.

God calls the boy by name, and three times Samuel runs to Eli, sure it was the old man's voice. He did 'not yet know the LORD,' nor how He speaks. A willing heart that runs to obey will, in time, learn whose voice is calling it.

God calls the boy by name, and three times Samuel runs to Eli, sure it was the old man's voice. He did 'not yet know the LORD,' nor how He speaks. A willing heart that runs to obey will, in time, learn whose voice is calling it.

God calls the boy by name, and three times Samuel runs to Eli, sure it was the old man's voice. He did 'not yet know the LORD,' nor how He speaks. A willing heart that runs to obey will, in time, learn whose voice is calling it.

God calls the boy by name, and three times Samuel runs to Eli, sure it was the old man's voice. He did 'not yet know the LORD,' nor how He speaks. A willing heart that runs to obey will, in time, learn whose voice is calling it.

At last Eli perceives that the LORD is calling the child and teaches him to answer, 'Speak, LORD; for thy servant heareth.' Failing as a father, Eli still points Samuel to God. Here is the whole secret of hearing God: a servant's heart, ready to listen and obey.

At last Eli perceives that the LORD is calling the child and teaches him to answer, 'Speak, LORD; for thy servant heareth.' Failing as a father, Eli still points Samuel to God. Here is the whole secret of hearing God: a servant's heart, ready to listen and obey.

The LORD comes and stands, and Samuel says, 'Speak; for thy servant heareth.' But the first word he hears is hard — judgment on the house of the man who raised him. To listen to God is to be ready for the difficult word as well as the comforting one.

The LORD comes and stands, and Samuel says, 'Speak; for thy servant heareth.' But the first word he hears is hard — judgment on the house of the man who raised him. To listen to God is to be ready for the difficult word as well as the comforting one.

Samuel fears to tell the vision, but Eli draws it out, and the boy hides nothing. Eli bows: 'It is the LORD: let him do what seemeth him good.' A faithful messenger speaks the whole truth, and a humbled heart submits to God's righteous will.

Samuel fears to tell the vision, but Eli draws it out, and the boy hides nothing. Eli bows: 'It is the LORD: let him do what seemeth him good.' A faithful messenger speaks the whole truth, and a humbled heart submits to God's righteous will.

Samuel grew, and the LORD 'let none of his words fall to the ground' — the sure mark of a true prophet. All Israel knew God had raised one up, and at Shiloh the LORD revealed Himself again. After long silence, His word returns through a faithful servant.
